prs.body: ## Summary: This PR related to issue #54789 updates the `URL` and `URLSearchParams` definitions in `globals.d.ts` to include missing standard properties and methods that are already supported by the runtime implementation. **Motivation:** I noticed that while the runtime supports standard URL operations (backed by `whatwg-url`), the TypeScript definitions were incomplete, causing unnecessary type errors when using standard Web APIs. ## Changelog: [GENERAL] [FIXED] - Added missing standard properties to `URL` (hash, host, pathname, etc.) and methods to `URLSearchParams` (get, set, delete, etc.). ## Test Plan: 1. Open `globals.d.ts`. 2. Verify that `URL` interface now includes all standard properties. 3. Verify that `URLSearchParams` interface now includes all standard methods. 4. Verify that no new TypeScript errors are introduced. Screenshot: Before making changes After making changes ![Uploading Screenshot 2025-12-04 at 11.39.42 PM.png…]()

prs.body: ## Summary: This PR updates the FormData class definition to include missing standard methods: delete, get, has, set, and forEach. **Motivation:** I was working on a project for my college coursework and ran into some TypeScript errors when trying to use standard FormData methods like get() and delete(), so I decided to fix it. ## Changelog: [GENERAL] [FIXED] - Added delete, get, has, set, and forEach to FormData type definition. ## Test Plan: 1. Open the globals type definition file. 2. Verify that the FormData class now includes the new methods: * delete(name: string): void; * get(name: string): FormDataValue | null; * has(name: string): boolean; * set(name: string, value: any): void; * forEach(...) 3. Verify that existing methods (append, getAll, getParts) are preserved. 4. Verify that no new TypeScript errors are introduced in the file. Screenshot:

prs.title: Authentication Flow & Implement Secure HttpOnly Cookie Login
prs.body: This PR completes the integration of the Login/Signup system between the frontend and backend using JWT HttpOnly cookies for secure authentication. It replaces the outdated token-in-localStorage flow and updates both frontend logic and backend configurations to ensure proper session handling. ✅ What Was Added / Fixed 🔧 Frontend (client) Updated LoginSignup.jsx to match backend response structure Removed incorrect { user, token } destructuring Now correctly reads res.data as the user object Removed unused token logic (token comes via HttpOnly cookie) Stores only userId + user in localStorage Added cleaner error handling for both login and signup Updated axios instance to use: withCredentials: true VITE_API_URL from environment variables Sign-up route updated from /register → /signup 🛠 Backend (api) Ensured CORS is correctly configured with: credentials: true origin: http://localhost:5173 Verified cookie-based JWT setup in auth.controller.js Cookies now contain token securely via httpOnly: true Returned user object sanitized (no password field) Verified verifyToken middleware correctly extracts token from cookies Minor cleanup in error responses for better debugging 🔒 Security Improvements Authentication now fully relies on HttpOnly cookies (XSS-safe) Removed all attempts to store JWT in client-side storage Backend token verification handled through middleware (verifyToken) 🧪 Manual Testing Performed ✓ Successful Signup → user stored in DB, cookie set, redirected to /dashboard ✓ Successful Login → cookie received, ProtectedRoute works ✓ Invalid login/signup shows correct error message ✓ CORS verified (Signup + Login both working cross-origin) ✓ Database connection verified and user creation confirmed 📦 Impact This PR finalizes the authentication system for the MVP phase and unlocks integration of: Protected dashboard routes User profile & membership features Club & event actions under authenticated sessions ss:

prs.title: Authentication Flow + Protected Dashboard Integration
prs.body:
This PR implements the complete authentication flow for the client application, including login/signup UI, backend integration, route protection, and dashboard redirection. It establishes a functional user experience from account creation → authentication → personalized dashboard view.

✅ What’s Included

Login & Signup UI (LoginSignup Component)
Added fully designed authentication screen with:

Login / Signup toggle

Password + Confirm Password logic

Show/Hide password functionality

Responsive UI with animations and modern design

API Integration
Added axios client (client/src/api/axios.js)

Connected frontend to backend auth endpoints:

POST /auth/login

POST /auth/register

Stores:

userId

token (if provided)
into localStorage on success

Routing Setup (React Router)
Added main application routing:

/auth → Login/Signup page

/dashboard → User dashboard

Cleaned and configured routes for predictable navigation.

Protected Routes
Implemented ProtectedRoute wrapper:

Blocks access to /dashboard unless localStorage.userId exists

Redirects unauthorized users to /auth

Dashboard Integration
Dashboard now:

Reads userId from localStorage

Fetches user data from /api/users/:id

Displays basic user information (name, role, club count, event count)

Vite / Client Fixes
Installed missing dependencies (axios)

Resolved Vite "Outdated optimize dep" and import analysis errors

Cleaned up folder structure (client/src/components/LoginSignup.jsx and client/src/api/axios.js)

🎯 Result

Users can now:

Create an account or log in

Get authenticated through backend

Automatically navigate to their dashboard

View personalized user data

Stay protected from direct /dashboard access without login

Functional login-to-dashboard flow is now complete.

🧪 Testing Steps

Start backend:
npm run start (in /api)

Start frontend:
npm run dev (in /client)

Visit /auth and:

Create an account

Login with credentials

Confirm automatic redirect to /dashboard

Clear localStorage and try accessing /dashboard → should redirect to /auth.

prs.title: fixed prisma
prs.body: This PR fixes an issue where Prisma threw the error: Argument `id`: Invalid value provided. Expected Int, provided String. The root cause was that req.params.id is always received as a string, but the Prisma schema defines User.id as an Int. This mismatch caused failures in findUnique, update, and delete operations. ✅ What’s Fixed 1. Converted all incoming route parameters (req.params.id) to integers Updated: getSingleUser deleteUser updateUser getUserDashboard Using: const id = Number(req.params.id); Added validation: if (Number.isNaN(id)) return next(createError(400, "Invalid user id")); 2. Ensured consistent ID type matching with Prisma schema Your schema defines: id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) All relevant Prisma queries now correctly use numeric IDs: where: { id } 3. Cleaned up and hardened controller logic Added type-checking and error handling for invalid IDs. Standardized integer parsing across all controllers. Updated password hashing during user creation. 🔍 Why This Matters Prevents Prisma runtime errors. Ensures reliable CRUD operations for user routes. Makes API behavior consistent with your database schema. Improves stability for frontend integrations. 🧪 Testing Tested endpoints: GET /api/users/:id DELETE /api/users/:id PUT /api/users/:id GET /api/users/:id/dashboard All now work correctly when passing numeric IDs.

prs.title: Implement User Dashboard (Backend + Frontend Integration)
prs.body: This Pull Request introduces the User Dashboard feature #9 , enabling the application to fetch and display detailed user information, including profile data, joined clubs, and registered events. It connects the backend Prisma-powered API with a new frontend dashboard page in React. 🔧 Backend Enhancements 1. Added Dashboard Endpoint A new route has been created to retrieve aggregated user data: GET /api/users/:id/dashboard 2. Controller Logic Fetches user details using Prisma Includes related: Club memberships Event registrations Returns a structured JSON response suitable for the frontend 3. Added Dummy User Creation (POST /api/users) Implemented createUser to simplify development/testing with mock data. 4. Improved Error Handling Graceful responses for invalid or missing user IDs Standardized error structure using createError utility 🎨 Frontend Additions 1. New Component: Dashboard.jsx Fetches dashboard data on mount Displays: User profile Joined clubs Registered events 2. API Integration Uses Axios to fetch: /api/users/:id/dashboard Connected via VITE_API_URL. 3. Routing Setup Added /dashboard route in App.jsx. 🧪 Testing Steps Start backend: npm run dev Create a test user via Postman (POST /api/users) Open frontend: npm run dev Visit: http://localhost:5173/dashboard Verify that: User data loads Clubs & events appear if present ✔️ Ready For Merge Backend API tested React dashboard loads without errors No breaking changes Fully functional with dummy data Screenshot:

prs.title: Descriptive readme.md
prs.body:
#13
Created a detailed and descriptive README.md file for the project.

prs.title: Add JSON import/export (download + import with validation)
prs.body:
Summary
Add JSON export/import support so users can backup, transfer, and share resume data. This patch implements a small utility for downloading resume JSON and validating/importing JSON files, plus UI wiring in the editor to export the current resume and import a JSON file. Imported data replaces the current resume state (without polluting undo history).
How to test
Start dev server:
npm run dev
In the editor:
Make changes to the resume and click "Export JSON" → confirm a .json file downloads.
Click "Import JSON" and select the exported file → confirm the editor updates to the imported state and shows a success alert.
Try importing malformed JSON or a file with missing/incorrect fields → confirm a helpful error alert.
Verify that import does not add to undo history (Undo should not undo the import).
Run lint:
npm run lint — should pass (I ran lint during development).

prs.title: PR title 🎚️ Centralize font-size scale — extract to constants.js and update templates
prs.body:
Extract the font-size scale used by all templates into a single shared constant so font sizing remains consistent when switching templates. This change centralizes the small/medium/large base/heading/subheading values in constants.js and updates each template to import and use the shared scale.

Problem
Each template previously defined its own font-size map. That caused inconsistent text sizing when users switched templates and made it harder to maintain or tweak typography site-wide.
What I changed
Added a shared constant:
constants.js — exports FONT_SIZES (small/medium/large → base/heading/subheading) and a default constants object.
Updated all templates to use the shared scale:
ClassicTemplate.js
ModernTemplate.js
MinimalTemplate.js
CreativeTemplate.js
No visual changes are intended aside from making sizes consistent across templates. The templates now pull sizes = FONT_SIZES[fontSize] instead of using local fontSizes objects.
Why this approach
Centralizing the scale makes it easy to:
Keep typography consistent across templates.
Adjust base/heading/subheading values from one place for all templates.
Add future theming or per-template overrides in a controlled way.
Kept the change minimal and dependency-free to simplify reviews.
How to test
Run the dev server:
npm run dev
Open the editor and switch between templates (Classic, Modern, Minimal, Creative).
Verify:
Text sizes for base, heading and subheading are consistent where expected for the selected fontSize (small/medium/large).
No runtime errors occur and pages render correctly.
Optionally, toggle customization.fontSize in the UI and verify sizes change accordingly across templates.

prs.title: Add undo/redo history with keyboard shortcuts and 20-entry limit
prs.body:
Add an undo/redo history to the editor so users can recover accidentally deleted or edited experience/education entries. This patch introduces a small dependency-free history hook, integrates Undo/Redo UI controls, binds standard keyboard shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl+Z and Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+Z), and caps the history at 20 snapshots to limit memory usage.

What changed
Added a lightweight history hook:
useResumeHistory.js — a small undo/redo manager that stores snapshots, supports functional updates, and enforces a configurable cap (default: 20).
Integrated history + controls into the editor:
index.js
Replaced the data.
Added "Undo" and "Redo" buttons to the header (disabled when not available).
Bound keyboard shortcuts:
Cmd/Ctrl+Z → undo
Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+Z → redo
Used replace() when loading sample or resetting so these actions do not pollute the history.
How to test
Start the app:
npm run dev
Open the editor and make changes:
Add/edit/remove experience or education items.
Undo/Redo via UI:
Click the "Undo" button to revert the last change.
Click "Redo" to reapply.
Undo/Redo via keyboard:
On macOS: Cmd+Z to undo, Shift+Cmd+Z to redo.
On Windows/Linux: Ctrl+Z to undo, Shift+Ctrl+Z to redo.
Verify Reset / Load sample:
Click Reset or Load sample — they should not create history entries (i.e., Undo should not immediately revert a Reset).
Verify history cap:
Make > 20 small changes (e.g., type or add/remove 25 times).
Ensure Undo still works for the most recent 20 actions and older ones are dropped.
Run lint:
npm run lint — should pass (I ran lint during development).

prs.title: fix(creative-template): make text theme-aware so content is readable in bright mode
prs.body: Summary Fixes an issue where text in the "Creative" resume template appeared white and unreadable when the app was in bright (light) mode. The template previously forced white text via an inline root color, which conflicted with the light background What I changed Updated CreativeTemplate.js: Removed the hard-coded root color: '#fff'. Added theme-aware Tailwind classes (e.g. text-slate-900 dark:text-slate-100) on the root container and key text elements. Replaced text-black usages with text-slate-900 and added dark: variants for consistent behavior across themes. Added text-slate-800 dark:text-slate-200 (and similar) to description/detail blocks so body text is readable in both modes. Kept dynamic primaryColor inline styles intact (they are computed and expected). How to test Install and run the dev server: npm install npm run dev Open the app in a browser. Ensure the app is in bright mode . Select the "Creative" template and verify: The "About Me" / Summary text is dark and readable. Experience details, education entries, and contact name are readable. Toggle to dark mode and verify text becomes light and remains readable (no regressions). Spot-check dynamic color sections (badges, primary-accent elements) still render as expected.

prs.title: Canvas Templates & Starter Kits Library #47
prs.body:
#47 

Summary:
Adds a template library feature so users can start from pre-built canvases.
Provides a Template Gallery UI, TemplateCard previews, Save-as-Template dialog, TemplateLoader service, and a backend API to list and persist templates.
Includes two starter templates (SWOT Analysis, Business Model Canvas) with SVG thumbnails.

Why:
New users always start from a blank canvas; templates reduce onboarding friction.
Templates speed up common workflows (brainstorming, wireframes, diagrams).
Templates provide consistent layouts for teams and inspire best practices.

Frontend changes:
Added:
templates.js-starter TEMPLATE_LIBRARY (SWOT, BMC).
Gallery.jsx — full-screen gallery with search and category filter.
TemplateCard.jsx — card view for each template.
SaveAsTemplate.jsx — dialog to save current canvas to server.
templateLoader.js — load template into canvas and helper to create room.
Dashboard.jsx — added "Templates" action to open gallery.

Backend changes:
Added :
templates.py — blueprint exposing:
GET /api/v1/templates — list templates (optional ?category=)
POST /api/v1/templates — create template (requires auth)
-Registered blueprint in app.py.

How to run / test locally:
Backend
From repo root:
cd /Users/manish/25octpr/ResCanvas
source .venv/bin/activate  # if using the venv
pip install -r backend/requirements.txt
python3 backend/app.py
Verify:
GET :http://localhost:10010/api/v1/templates
POST : /api/v1/templates (requires auth token)
Frontend
cd frontend
npm install
npm start
In-app:
Dashboard → Templates → Choose a template → "Use Template"
(MVP) Selecting a template creates a new private room and navigates to it. Server-side seeding of the canvas is a follow-up item.

prs.title: Template Customization #4
prs.body: Summary This PR makes the resume preview templates theme and templates theme -aware so the resume text appears dark in light mode and white in dark mode. It also ensures section headings ("Name", "Experience", "Education", "Skills", etc.) render with appropriate contrast while keeping existing accent colors (primaryColor) intact. Files Modify/Added : ClassicTemplate.js ModernTemplate.js MinimalTemplate.js CreativeTemplate.js

prs.title: feat(ui): add real-time validation, progress indicator, dark mode, and mobile/responsive improvements
prs.body:
#3 
Summary

Adds real-time validation for required contact fields (name, email) with inline error messages and accessibility attributes.
Adds a lightweight progress indicator and simple section navigation so users can see form completion and jump to sections.
Improves layout and small-screen spacing for better mobile usability.
Adds a dark mode toggle persisted to localStorage and powered by Tailwind class-based dark mode.
Small accessibility improvements .
Why

Improve form UX (instant feedback, reduce invalid input on export).
Help users complete long resumes via progress and section navigation.
Make the editor pleasant and accessible on phones and for users who prefer dark theme.
Files changed.

prs.title: set Send button background to #007fff
prs.body: issue #144 Summary - Update the AI Chatbot Send button color to the requested blue (#007fff) to improve visibility and match the design spec. What I changed - Changed `.sendbtn` background color in `wwwroot/css/app.css` from `#CFE7FF` to `#007fff`. Why - The new color improves contrast and aligns the Send button with the requested design/branding. Verification / How to test 1. Run the app locally (your usual dev steps). 2. Navigate to AI → LLM Vulnerabilities → open a lab (e.g., Prompt Injection). 3. Confirm the Send button is now the blue color #007fff. 4. If you don't see the change, clear the browser cache and restart the app (CSS may be cached). Files changed - `wwwroot/css/app.css` — update `.sendbtn` background to `#007fff` Notes - I avoided using `!important` to keep styles clean. If other styles override `.sendbtn` in some contexts, I can scope the selector more specifically or add a stronger rule. - If you prefer the color change scoped only to LLM/chat pages (so `.sendbtn` elsewhere is unaffected), I can update the CSS selector accordingly. Related - (Add issue/PR references here, e.g., fixes #)

prs.title: Add my new feature
prs.body:
# Pull Request

## Related Issue

Enable 'Enter' key to submit comments and replies #16.

## Summary of Changes

Enable users to submit comments or replies by pressing the Enter key in the input field, while optionally allowing Shift+Enter to insert a new line.

- Added/Modified/Fixed [ViewPostModal and Chat ]
- Implemented ["Enter" key to submit comments/replies/messages, "Shift+Enter" to insert a new line in input fields for improved user experience and accessibility]

## How to Test
1-Open the app and navigate to a post or chat where you can add a comment, reply, or message.
2-Click into the comment/reply or chat input field.
3-Type your message.
4-Press Enter: The message/comment/reply should be submitted immediately.
5-Type a message and press Shift+Enter:A new line should be inserted in the input field, not submitted.
6-Confirm that the Send/Submit button still works as before.
7-Repeat for both comment/reply and chat input areas to ensure consistent behavior.

## Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots to show the changes.

## Checklist

- [ yes] Code follows the style guidelines of this project.
- [ ] Tests have been added or updated.
- [ ] Documentation has been updated (if needed).
- [yes ] This PR resolves the associated issue.

## Additional Notes

Any additional information or context related to this PR.

issues.title: Missing standard properties and methods in URL and URLSearchParams type definitions

labels.name: Issue: Author Provided Repro
labels.description: This issue can be reproduced in Snack or an attached project.

labels.name: Resolution: Fixed
labels.description: A PR that fixes this issue has been merged.

issues.body: ### Description The current `URL` and `URLSearchParams` type definitions in `globals.d.ts` are missing several standard properties and methods that are present in the Web API and supported by the React Native runtime (via `whatwg-url` polyfill). For `URL`, properties like `hash`, `host`, `hostname`, `href`, `origin`, `password`, `pathname`, `port`, `protocol`, `search`, `username` are missing. For `URLSearchParams`, methods like `delete`, `get`, `getAll`, `has`, `set`, `sort`, `forEach`, `keys`, `values`, `entries` are missing. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a new React Native project with TypeScript. 2. Try to access `new URL('https://example.com').pathname`. 3. Try to use `new URLSearchParams().get('key')`. 4. Observe TypeScript errors: `Property 'pathname' does not exist on type 'URL'` and `Property 'get' does not exist on type 'URLSearchParams'`. ### React Native Version 0.82.0 ### Affected Platforms Runtime - Android, Runtime - iOS ### Output of `npx @react-native-community/cli info` ```text N/A - Static Analysis Issue (Type Definition Bug) ``` ### Stacktrace or Logs ```text Property 'pathname' does not exist on type 'URL'. Property 'get' does not exist on type 'URLSearchParams'. ``` ### MANDATORY Reproducer https://snack.expo.dev/@manishbalayan/intelligent-indigo-apples?platform=ios ### Screenshots and Videos There is the error shown in Screenshot

issues.title: Missing standard methods in FormData type definition

labels.name: Needs: Repro
labels.description: This issue could be improved with a clear list of steps to reproduce the issue.

issues.body: ### Description The current `FormData` type definition in `globals.d.ts` is missing several standard methods that are present in the Web API. This causes TypeScript errors when trying to use methods like `delete`, `get`, `has`, `set`, or `forEach` on a `FormData` object. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a new React Native project with TypeScript. 2. Try to use `formData.get('key')` or `formData.delete('key')`. 3. Observe the TypeScript error: `Property 'get' does not exist on type 'FormData'`. ### React Native Version 0.76.0 ### Affected Platforms Runtime - Android, Runtime - iOS ### Output of `npx @react-native-community/cli info` ```text N/A - Static Analysis Issue (Type Definition Bug) ``` ### Stacktrace or Logs ```text Property 'get' does not exist on type 'FormData'. Property 'delete' does not exist on type 'FormData'. Property 'set' does not exist on type 'FormData'. ``` ### MANDATORY Reproducer https://github.com/facebook/react-native/blob/main/packages/react-native/src/types/globals.d.ts ### Screenshots and Videos

issues.title: White text in Creative template not visible in bright mode
issues.body: When using the Creative resume template in bright mode, some text elements (e.g., in the “About Me” section) appear in white, making them nearly invisible against the light background. Steps to Reproduce: Open the app in bright mode. Select the Creative template. Observe that some text (like the description under “About Me”) is white and unreadable. Expected Behavior: Text should adjust to a darker color in bright mode for proper visibility.

issues.title: Advanced Case Conversion Features Hidden from Users

labels.name: wocs
labels.description: Particapting in Winter of Code Social

issues.body:
🐞 Issue Description

Three advanced case conversion functions (Title Case, Toggle Case, Alternating Case) are fully implemented in the codebase but are not accessible through the UI. Users cannot access these features even though they're working in the backend.



✅ Expected Behavior

Users should be able to access all implemented text transformation features through the toolbar, including Title Case (capitalizes first letter of each major word), Toggle Case (flips the case of every character), and Alternating Case (creates aLtErNaTiNg CaSe text).



⚙️ Steps To Reproduce

1. Go to the WordWizard home page
2. Look at the toolbar buttons above the textarea
3. Notice only "Convert to uppercase" and "Convert to lowercase" options are available
4. Check the source code src/data/textUtils.js lines 169-204 - three more case conversion functions exist
5. Check src/components/Toolbar.jsx lines 24-43 - these functions are not mapped to toolbar icons



📸 Screenshots

Functions exist in textUtils.js: handleTitleCase(), handleToggleCase(), handleAlternatingCase() but are missing from ICON_MAP in Toolbar.jsx.

🧠 Additional Context

The functions are complete and working. They need to be added to the ICON_MAP in Toolbar.jsx with appropriate icons from lucide-react and added to the ALT_TEXT object for accessibility.

issues.title: Text Formatting Buttons Don't Visually Apply Styles
issues.body:
🐞 Issue Description

The text formatting buttons (Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough) in the toolbar toggle their active state but don't actually apply visual formatting to the text in the textarea. Users click the buttons, they highlight as "active," but the text appearance doesn't change.



✅ Expected Behavior

When users click the Bold, Italic, Underline, or Strikethrough buttons, the text in the textarea should visually reflect these formatting changes. Bold text should appear bolder, italic text should be slanted, underlined text should have an underline, and strikethrough text should have a line through it.

⚙️ Steps To Reproduce

1. Go to the WordWizard home page
2. Type some text in the textarea (e.g., "Hello World")
3. Click the Bold button in the toolbar
4. Observe that the button becomes active/highlighted but the text does not appear bold
5. Try clicking Italic, Underline, or Strikethrough buttons - none visually change the text



📸 Screenshots

The computed textDecoration variable exists in src/components/TextForm.jsx lines 113-121 but is never applied to the textarea's style prop (lines 147-156).

🧠 Additional Context

The state management exists (isBold, isItalic, isUnderline, isStrike) and is tracked correctly. The fix requires applying fontWeight, fontStyle, and textDecoration CSS properties to the textarea based on these state values.

issues.title: Text Formatting Buttons Don't Visually Apply Styles
issues.body:
🐞 Issue Description
The text formatting buttons (Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough) in the toolbar toggle their active state but don't actually apply visual formatting to the text in the textarea. The textDecoration variable is computed in TextForm.jsx (lines 113-121) but is never used in the textarea's style prop.

✅ Expected Behavior
When users click the Bold, Italic, Underline, or Strikethrough buttons, the text in the textarea should visually reflect these formatting changes:
•  Bold text should appear bolder
•  Italic text should be slanted
•  Underlined text should have an underline
•  Strikethrough text should have a line through it

⚙️ Steps To Reproduce
1. Go to the home page
2. Type some text in the textarea (e.g., "Hello World")
3. Click the Bold button in the toolbar
4. Observe that the button becomes active/highlighted but the text does not appear bold
5. Try clicking Italic, Underline, or Strikethrough buttons
6. Notice none of them visually change the text appearance

📸 Screenshots
Check src/components/TextForm.jsx:147-156 - the textarea element doesn't have any dynamic style applied for formatting states.

🧠 Additional Context
The issue is that while state management exists (isBold, isItalic, isUnderline, isStrike), the computed textDecoration value needs to be applied to the textarea. Add fontWeight, fontStyle, and textDecoration to the textarea's style prop.

issues.title: Missing i18n Translations for About Page Content
issues.body:
•  File: src/data/accordionItems.js
•  Issue: The accordion items are hardcoded in English and not using the i18n translation system, unlike other components
•  Impact: When user switches to Hindi, the About page accordion content remains in English
•  Related to: Issue #101 mentions Hindi toggle doesn't translate About page - this is the root cause
•  Severity: Medium

issues.title: Template does not reset to default “Sample” view after selection
issues.body:
When clicking on a template in the resume.ly app, the selected template preview loads correctly. However, when trying to reset or return to the default “Sample” view, the template does not revert. It stays stuck on the previously selected template.

Steps to Reproduce:

Open the app at (https://resumeely.netlify.app/).
Select any template (e.g., “Classic”).
Click “Reset” or attempt to return to the default “Sample” template.
Expected Behavior:
The preview should return to the default “Sample” layout when reset or deselected.

Actual Behavior:
The selected template remains active, and the sample view does not reload.

Environment:
Browser: Brave
OS: macOS
URL: [resumeley.netlify.app](https://resumely.netlify.app/)
